nodes.label: services/orion-spark-concept-induction/README.md — bus-native Spark capability that consolidates recent Orion experience into concept profiles and deltas, then publishes them on the Titanium bus
nodes.norm_label: services/orion-spark-concept-induction/readme.md — bus-native spark capability that consolidates recent orion experience into concept profiles and deltas, then publishes them on the titanium bus
nodes.community_name: services/orion-memory-consolidation/README.md — subscribes to orion:memory:turn:persisted, classifies each chat turn via LLM gateway quick-lane logprobs, patches chat_history_log.spark_meta, tracks consolidation windows, and on boundary closure runs a deterministic consolidation gate (default) or legacy graph suggest

nodes.label: DriveEngine — this service's drive_state.v1 computation, persisted to a local LocalProfileStore JSON file at CONCEPT_STORE_PATH
nodes.norm_label: driveengine — this service's drive_state.v1 computation, persisted to a local localprofilestore json file at concept_store_path
nodes.community_name: services/orion-memory-consolidation/README.md — subscribes to orion:memory:turn:persisted, classifies each chat turn via LLM gateway quick-lane logprobs, patches chat_history_log.spark_meta, tracks consolidation windows, and on boundary closure runs a deterministic consolidation gate (default) or legacy graph suggest

nodes.label: scripts/drive_state_divergence_audit.py — report-only diagnostic comparing drive_state.v1 (this service's DriveEngine, JSON file) against autonomy_state_v2 (orion.autonomy.reducer, Postgres), both independently computing pressures over the same 6-key drive taxonomy and marked DUPLICATE of each other in orion/self_state/inner_state_registry.py; never merges or picks a winner, always exits 0
nodes.norm_label: scripts/drive_state_divergence_audit.py — report-only diagnostic comparing drive_state.v1 (this service's driveengine, json file) against autonomy_state_v2 (orion.autonomy.reducer, postgres), both independently computing pressures over the same 6-key drive taxonomy and marked duplicate of each other in orion/self_state/inner_state_registry.py; never merges or picks a winner, always exits 0
nodes.community_name: services/orion-memory-consolidation/README.md — subscribes to orion:memory:turn:persisted, classifies each chat turn via LLM gateway quick-lane logprobs, patches chat_history_log.spark_meta, tracks consolidation windows, and on boundary closure runs a deterministic consolidation gate (default) or legacy graph suggest

nodes.label: Satisfaction tensions (drive relief): DriveEngine.update() now supports signed drive impacts — a negative drive_impacts weight relieves pressure instead of raising it, bounded to [-1,1] via _clamp_signed with relief bounded to [0, base] and growth to [base, 1]; previously negative weights were silently double-clamped to zero so drives could only ever accumulate, never discharge, pressure
nodes.norm_label: satisfaction tensions (drive relief): driveengine.update() now supports signed drive impacts — a negative drive_impacts weight relieves pressure instead of raising it, bounded to [-1,1] via _clamp_signed with relief bounded to [0, base] and growth to [base, 1]; previously negative weights were silently double-clamped to zero so drives could only ever accumulate, never discharge, pressure
nodes.community_name: services/orion-memory-consolidation/README.md — subscribes to orion:memory:turn:persisted, classifies each chat turn via LLM gateway quick-lane logprobs, patches chat_history_log.spark_meta, tracks consolidation windows, and on boundary closure runs a deterministic consolidation gate (default) or legacy graph suggest

nodes.label: orion:autonomy:action:outcome subscription — published by Layer-9 dispatch on every autonomous action (see services/orion-execution-dispatch-runtime); extract_tensions_from_action_outcome mints a relief tension when outcome.success is True for a closed set of dispatch kinds (inspect relieves coherence, summarize relieves predictive, observe relieves continuity); known accepted risk that this bus-emitted-success path and the Postgres-polled-failure path can independently fire a growth and a relief tension for the same event, not coordinated in this patch
nodes.norm_label: orion:autonomy:action:outcome subscription — published by layer-9 dispatch on every autonomous action (see services/orion-execution-dispatch-runtime); extract_tensions_from_action_outcome mints a relief tension when outcome.success is true for a closed set of dispatch kinds (inspect relieves coherence, summarize relieves predictive, observe relieves continuity); known accepted risk that this bus-emitted-success path and the postgres-polled-failure path can independently fire a growth and a relief tension for the same event, not coordinated in this patch
nodes.community_name: services/orion-execution-dispatch-runtime/README.md — Layer 9 of the Orion cognition substrate: converts PolicyDecisionFrameV1 + ProposalFrameV1 + SelfStateV1 into ExecutionDispatchFrameV1 envelopes, the motor-nerve service that can actually send real actions

nodes.label: Readonly capabilities (recall.query.readonly, P4): ConceptWorker is the sole production call site for maybe_execute_substrate_act_after_metabolism, which gates a Firecrawl fetch and a new inline RecallService RPC under config/autonomy/capability_policy.v1.yaml per cycle, trying recall first so a successful recall leaves that cycle's fetch budget unconsumed; degrades to a no-op if recall_bus/recall_source kwargs aren't wired
nodes.norm_label: readonly capabilities (recall.query.readonly, p4): conceptworker is the sole production call site for maybe_execute_substrate_act_after_metabolism, which gates a firecrawl fetch and a new inline recallservice rpc under config/autonomy/capability_policy.v1.yaml per cycle, trying recall first so a successful recall leaves that cycle's fetch budget unconsumed; degrades to a no-op if recall_bus/recall_source kwargs aren't wired
nodes.community_name: config/autonomy/capability_policy.v1.yaml — policy config gating which autonomy capabilities may auto-execute per cycle, by side-effect class, required goal status, required drive origins/signal kinds, and per-cycle budget

nodes.label: BUS_INTAKE_CHANNELS footgun: ConceptSettings reads this via a validation_alias that fully replaces, not merges, the Python default channel list — adding a new intake channel to the code default alone does nothing in any environment where the env var is already set (every .env_example and deployed .env sets it), so the new channel must be added to every env file or the subscription silently no-ops
nodes.norm_label: bus_intake_channels footgun: conceptsettings reads this via a validation_alias that fully replaces, not merges, the python default channel list — adding a new intake channel to the code default alone does nothing in any environment where the env var is already set (every .env_example and deployed .env sets it), so the new channel must be added to every env file or the subscription silently no-ops
